channel.title: Digg AI Feed from X
channel.description: Top 10 AI stories from Digg's X-ranked tech feed with click-through links to original source content.

item.title: DeepSeek releases DSpark, a speculative decoding algorithm boosting DeepSeek-V4 throughput by up to 661%
item.description: Open-source vLLM has rapidly integrated support for the algorithm.
item.content:encoded:
Open-source vLLM has rapidly integrated support for the algorithm.

Original source: https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/46995

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/47usplg9

DeepSeek has introduced DSpark, a speculative decoding algorithm that attaches a lightweight draft module to existing V4 model weights and delivers substantial inference acceleration while keeping the original output distribution intact. The technique combines a parallel draft backbone with a sequential head and a hardware-aware scheduler, and early production numbers show it already running inside DeepSeek’s own serving stack. 23 579 61 100 35.8K ZE #1695 | @ZEPHYR_Z9 T( #501 | @TEORTAXESTEX KA #403 | @YACINEMTB

  • Views: 4,070
  • Likes: 11
  • Bookmarks: 3
  • Replies: 3
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • kache
  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • Zephyr
DSpark will see crazy adoption The Whale reduced the compute pressure on Chinese AI infra

item.title: Alibaba's Qwen team disputes researcher Tianhang Zhu's claims of leading its reinforcement learning development
item.description: Zhu's Google Scholar profile listed papers lacking his name.
item.content:encoded:
Zhu's Google Scholar profile listed papers lacking his name.

Original source: https://www.linkedin.com/in/bobzhu

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/s41zyhe3

A public back-and-forth erupted on X after Tianhang Zhu posted about his new role leading LLM research at Fundamental Research Labs while referencing his past Alibaba Qwen work. Team members pushed back, stating he left after the first Qwen model and that no head-of-RL position existed on the team at the time. 17 96 1 5 8.4K T( #501 | @TEORTAXESTEX SZ #84 | @SUCHENZANG

  • Views: 3,912
  • Likes: 49
  • Bookmarks: 4
  • Replies: 10
  • Posts: 7

Top authors

  • Susan Zhang
  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
The plot thickens! I guess Tianhang can boast of his RL contributions very legitimately. PPO back then was a really high-risk, high-effort choice. I apologize.

item.title: DeepSeek open-sources core ML infrastructure projects DeepEP and DeepGEMM to optimize throughput and lower inference costs
item.description: Nvidia's Dynamo infrastructure reportedly responds directly to these releases
item.content:encoded:
Nvidia's Dynamo infrastructure reportedly responds directly to these releases

Original source: https://x.com/Xianbao_QIAN/status/2071917185380073611

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/8ranai60

DeepSeek has released two production-focused libraries under MIT licenses on GitHub: DeepEP for efficient expert-parallel communication in MoE setups and DeepGEMM for high-performance GEMM kernels with FP8/FP4 support and fused operations. The tools target inference bottlenecks with near-bandwidth throughput, reduced SM occupancy in later versions, and easy PyTorch integration via lightweight JIT compilation on Hopper-class GPUs or newer. 1 9 1 3 2.9K ZE #1695 | @ZEPHYR_Z9 T( #501 | @TEORTAXESTEX

  • Views: 2,736
  • Likes: 7
  • Bookmarks: 2
  • Replies: 1
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • Zephyr
Nvidia Dynamo exists because of the whale

item.title: AI developer teortaxesTex argues China must spend $1.5 trillion annually to match US AI resource intensity
item.description: Hardware costs cap China's data center savings at 10%.
item.content:encoded:
Hardware costs cap China's data center savings at 10%.

Original source: https://x.com/teortaxesTex/status/2071927594367213752

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/qo15l6pu

Pseudonymous AI commentator @teortaxesTex, known as a vocal DeepSeek supporter, lays out why China would need roughly $1.5 trillion per year in AI spending to reach the same resource intensity as the US, where raw private investment runs far higher though state support and cost differences shrink the effective gap to perhaps two or three times. 3 7 0 2 2.1K ZE #1695 | @ZEPHYR_Z9 T( #501 | @TEORTAXESTEX

  • Views: 2,078
  • Likes: 7
  • Bookmarks: 2
  • Replies: 3
  • Posts: 3

Top authors

  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • Zephyr
Memory+Compute+Networking is 80% of total CAPEX (China has no advantage here) The rest 20% is power+cooling+shell Assuming China is 50% cheaper compared to US The max net savings is 10%

item.title: Google DeepMind opens its new London headquarters, named Platform 37 after AlphaGo's historic Move 37
item.description: DeepMind staff shared first-day tours of the terraced workspace.
item.content:encoded:
DeepMind staff shared first-day tours of the terraced workspace.

Original source: https://x.com/fofrAI/status/2071883174603338212

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/84u6b971

Google DeepMind has begun moving staff into its first wholly owned headquarters outside the US, a purpose-built King’s Cross complex named Platform 37 that nods to both the adjacent rail station and AlphaGo’s famous 2016 move. Early tours show a terraced workspace already in use this summer, with the public AI Exchange space still slated for later in the year. 1 17 0 1 913 FO #1853 | @FOFRAI JP #774 | @JPARKERHOLDER

  • Views: 913
  • Likes: 17
  • Bookmarks: 1
  • Replies: 1
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Jack Parker-Holder
  • fofr
Even more inspiring, it’s coming home (personal view only 🤣)

item.title: ETH Zürich's Florian Tramèr questions ACL 2026 conference budget allocating $50 per coffee break with no actual meals
item.description: Organizers claim food costs will exceed $800 per attendee.
item.content:encoded:
Organizers claim food costs will exceed $800 per attendee.

Original source: https://x.com/florian_tramer/status/2071896295610990786/photo/1

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/lyjsa0jn

A leading researcher at ETH Zürich is calling out the ACL 2026 organizers over registration pricing that assigns roughly $50 to each of a dozen coffee breaks while providing no meals beyond a single welcome reception and one social event, even as total food and beverage costs are said to top $800 per attendee. 0 1 0 0 147 FT #1659 | @FLORIAN_TRAMER GK #197 | @THEGAUTAMKAMATH

  • Views: 147
  • Likes: 1
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Gautam Kamath
  • Florian Tramèr
@florian_tramer @aclmeeting Maybe it's because of all the vegans pumping up the catering costs? For example at last EACL we were provided with the ultimate catering experience: a plastic bowl of stale fruits (for coffee break snacks and lunch).
